_comment: Pre-Crime Deployment Manifest — copy this file, fill it in, then run: node deploy.js --manifest your-manifest.json

product.name: Name of what you are selling
product.description: 2-3 sentence description of the product or service. What it does. Who it's for. Why it's valuable.
differentiators: What makes you different from competitors — be specific
product.geography: Cities/regions you serve — e.g., 'Los Angeles metro area'
product.pricing: e.g., '$175/hour, one-hour deposit to hold date'

segments.label: Human-readable segment name
events: What occasion or need creates a purchase decision

high: phrase or keyword that strongly signals buying intent
e.g. 'looking for entertainment' or 'prom' or 'holiday party'
medium: phrase that signals relevance but not urgency
timing: phrase that signals an event is being planned
e.g. 'save the date' or 'mark your calendars' or 'annual'
not: Topic or content type that is NOT relevant

factletTopics: Broad industry or audience topic worth tracking in RSS feeds
e.g. 'corporate event planning trends' or 'student mental health policy'
factletNotTopics: Topics that are NOT worth factleting
e.g. 'individual school sports results' or 'college admissions'

criteria.0: No contact found or no email
criteria.1: Generic inbox (info@, office@) or role unclear
criteria.2: Named decision-maker with direct personal email

criteria.1: Historical evidence (past event, past purchase pattern)
criteria.2: Active buying signal (job posting, announcement, stated need)

criteria.1: Indirect/inferred pain (sector pressure, typical challenges for this org type)
criteria.2: Direct/observed pain (stated problem, visible gap, complaint, urgent deadline)

criteria.0: No usable intel found (no website, no social, no news)
criteria.1: Scraped something useful (org profile, recent activity, decision-maker context)

criteria.0: Only generic info (org name, address) — no differentiating facts
criteria.1: At least one fact unique to THIS client (named event, specific statement, recent news)

criteria.0: Nothing specific enough to open an email with
criteria.1: Clear, specific hook exists (event, pain point, quote, timing)
At least two independent sources (website + social, website + news, etc.)

hardGates: No email at all → warmthScore = 0, draftStatus = brewing, do not compose
Client outside service geography → warmthScore = 0, mark brewing, note as OUT_OF_GEOGRAPHY

evaluatorCriteria.name: Specificity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft reference something specific to THIS client — not 'organizations like yours'?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: References a named event, stated problem, specific initiative, or recent action unique to them
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: 'Companies like yours often face...' / 'As a [role type], you probably deal with...'

evaluatorCriteria.name: Recency
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft reference something from the past 2 years?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: References a data point from 2024 or later — post, job listing, news, announcement
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Only references static permanent facts with no timely hook

evaluatorCriteria.name: Pain-to-Product Bridge
evaluatorCriteria.description: Does the draft connect THEIR situation to the product in one clear sentence?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: One sentence that links their specific pain to a specific product capability
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Lists product features without connecting to their situation OR describes their pain without the bridge

evaluatorCriteria.name: Brevity
evaluatorCriteria.description: Under the word limit. Every sentence earns its place.
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Over limit, or contains any filler phrase from the forbidden list

evaluatorCriteria.name: Reply Test
evaluatorCriteria.description: Would a busy decision-maker reply to this?
evaluatorCriteria.passExample: Earns 5 more seconds. They might forward it to a colleague.
evaluatorCriteria.failExample: Finger on delete by sentence two

outreachRules.tone: confident, direct, human — sounds like a person who did their homework
outreachRules.openWith: Lead with THEIR world — their event, their problem, their situation. Never with your name or product name.
outreachRules.closeWith: Close with a command, not a hope. 'Let's set up a call' not 'I hope to hear from you'
forbidden: I hope this finds you well

bookingConfig._comment: Applies only to lead-capture / booking deployments. Leave blank for pure outreach deployments.
bookingConfig.defaultTrade: Your trade category — e.g. DJ, Photographer, Caterer, Caricature Artist
_defaultBookingAction_options: leedz_api    — post to The Leedz marketplace via API (requires leedzEmail + leedzSession in Config)
email_share  — email booking to share@theleedz.com for manual submission
email_user   — email booking to companyEmail for seller to decide

additionalKeywords: keyword to add to global list
keyword to add to global list
